title: StateStore uses std RwLock but exposes write() guard to async callers risking deadlock across await
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/state/store.rs:110-131, 144-148
## Violation
StateStore wraps SessionState in std::sync::RwLock and hands out RwLockWriteGuard via write(). The module docs explicitly forbid holding the guard across .await but there is no compile-time enforcement.

```rust
//! 1. Never hold a read guard across an `.await`.
//! 2. Never hold a write guard across an `.await`.
```

Any caller that holds the guard across an await will deadlock or poison the lock. The docs say panics on poisoned lock — one mishandled await terminates all future state access for the process.

## Mandated refactor
Either switch to tokio::sync::RwLock or replace the write() guard API with a closure-passing with_write closure so the borrow cannot cross an .await by construction.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
